variants.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the tongue smell behind this animal behavior: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.seo_title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.yt_description: Snakes smell the air with their tongues. Watch the tongue smell first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick.
variants.script: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?

variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the grooming signal behind this animal behavior: Cats use whiskers like built-in rulers
variants.seo_title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
variants.yt_description: Cats groom to reset their scent map. Watch the grooming signal first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map.
variants.script: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map. Watch the tongue and paw movements, because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat. variants.title: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the motion lock behind this animal behavior: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision
variants.seo_title: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair
variants.yt_description: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair. Watch the motion lock first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision.
variants.script: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision. Watch the eyes before the flight path, because dragonflies process motion fast. They can predict where prey is going, then adjust in the air like a tiny interceptor. That is why the catch looks impossibly clean. Would you see it coming?
variants.lead: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision. Watch the eyes before the flight path, because dragonflies process motion fast. They can predict where prey is going, then adjust in the air like a tiny interceptor. That is why the catch looks impossibly clean. Would you see it coming?

variants.seo_title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder

variants.hook: Lightning turns air into a shock wave.
variants.script: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?
